compressed_prompt: Create an elaborate java learning session plan. Include a postscript beginning with 'P.P.S'. No commas in formal tone.
response:
**JAVA LEARNING SESSION PLAN**

**Session Title:** Foundational Java Principles & Object-Oriented Paradigms

**Primary Objective:** To establish a robust understanding of core Java syntax and object-oriented programming concepts.

**Duration:** Three Hours

**Structure:**

**I. Preliminaries (00:15)**
*   Participant System Verification
*   Installation of Required JDK and IDE
*   Environment Variable Configuration Check
*   Statement of Learning Goals

**II. Core Syntax & Data Handling (00:45)**
*   Analysis of Basic Program Structure
*   Declaration and Initialization of Primitive Data Types
*   Exploration of String Class and Its Immutability
*   Implementation of Basic Control Flow Constructs
*   Practical Exercise: Building a Simple Calculator Logic

**III. Object-Oriented Programming Foundations (01:00)**
*   Definition and Design of Custom Classes
*   Distinction Between Instance and Static Members
*   Construction and Overloading of Constructors
*   Demonstration of Encapsulation with Access Modifiers
*   Practical Exercise: Creating a Student Information Management Class

**IV. Advanced OOP Concepts (01:00)**
*   Principles of Inheritance and Hierarchical Class Design
*   Utilization of Polymorphism Through Method Overriding
*   Introduction to Abstract Classes and Interface Contracts
*   Practical Exercise: Implementing a Shape Hierarchy with Area Calculation

**Post-Session Requirements:**
*   Review of all written code examples
*   Completion of assigned programming problems
*   Preparation of questions for the subsequent session on Exception Handling and Collections

**P.P.S** All code written during this session must be annotated with precise comments. Participants should archive their work in a designated version control repository.
